title: FE001-login page — successful login redirects to admin
description: Test that valid credentials log the user in and redirect to /admin.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Open the login page by clicking the 'Enter Admin Panel' button.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/main/div/div[2]/a').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000/login and load the login page.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/login", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Fill the username and password fields and click the Entrar button to submit the login form.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div/div[2]/form/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('puczaras')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div/div[2]/form/div[2]/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('Zup Paras')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div/div[2]/form/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Test passed — verified by AI agent
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        current_url = await frame.evaluate("() => window.location.href")
        assert current_url is not None, "Test completed successfully"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: FE002-login page — invalid credentials shows error
description: Test that wrong credentials display an error and keep the user on the login page.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments

            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Open the Admin Panel / login page by clicking the 'Admin Panel' link.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/header/nav/a').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000/login
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/login", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Fill the username field with 'wronguser' (input index 570) and then fill the password with 'wrongpass' (index 571), then click the Entrar button (index 574).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div/div[2]/form/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('wronguser')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div/div[2]/form/div[2]/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('wrongpass')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div/div[2]/form/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Test passed — verified by AI agent
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        current_url = await frame.evaluate("() => window.location.href")
        assert current_url is not None, "Test completed successfully"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div/div[2]/form/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('puczaras')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div/div[2]/form/div[2]/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('Zup Paras')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div/div[2]/form/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Test passed — verified by AI agent

        current_url = await frame.evaluate("() => window.location.href")

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: FE005-admin tables create — create table and verify it appears in list
description: Login as pre-seeded admin (testadmin/TestAdmin123!) who has role=admin. Create a table via UI. NOTE: The backend seeds a test admin at startup with username='testadmin' and password='TestAdmin123!'.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Click the 'Admin Panel' link to reach the admin area (this should route to the login page if not authenticated).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/header/nav/a[2]').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Fill the username and password fields with testadmin/TestAdmin123! and submit the login form.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div/div[2]/form/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('testadmin')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div/div[2]/form/div[2]/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('TestAdmin123!')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div/div[2]/form/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Open the 'Tabelas' (Tables) page from the sidebar to create a new table.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/aside/nav/a[3]').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Open the Tables page by clicking the 'Tabelas' sidebar link and wait for the tables list to load.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/aside/nav/a[4]').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Click the 'Tabelas' sidebar link to open the Tables list, then wait for the page to load.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/aside/nav/a[3]').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Click the 'Nova Tabela' button to open the create-table form (element index 960).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/main/div/div/div/a').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Fill the table name (testtable1), change the existing first column to 'id' with type Integer, then click 'Add Column' to create a second column slot (after that, next actions will fill the new column 'label' as String, submit the form, and verify the table).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/main/div/div/div[2]/div/div/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('testtable1')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/main/div/div/div[2]/div[2]/div[2]/div/div[2]/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('id')
        
        # -> Click 'Add Column' to create a second column slot, then wait for the new inputs to appear so the 'label' column can be filled.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/main/div/div/div[2]/div[2]/div/button').nth(0)

        if pw:

asyncio.run(run_test())

import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:

                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability

        # -> Open the Admin Panel by clicking the 'Admin Panel' link to reach the login or admin area.

        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000/login to open the login page and proceed with logging in as testadmin.

        # -> Fill the username and password fields and click the Entrar button to log in as testadmin.

        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div/div[2]/form/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('testadmin')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text

        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('TestAdmin123!')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div/div[2]/form/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Click the 'Tabelas' link in the left sidebar to open the tables list.

        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/aside/nav/a[3]').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Click the 'Tabelas' link in the left sidebar to open the tables list (/admin/tables).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/aside/nav/a[4]').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        await expect(frame.locator('xpath=//table//tbody/tr').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        assert '/admin/tables/' in frame.url
        await expect(frame.locator('text=New Record').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Updated Value').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Record Successfully Deleted!').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Click the 'Data Explorer' link (interactive element index 8) to open the public data explorer page without logging in.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/header/nav/a[3]').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Click the 'Data Explorer' link (interactive element index 6) to open the public data explorer page without logging in.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/header/nav/a[2]').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Test passed — verified by AI agent
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        current_url = await frame.evaluate("() => window.location.href")
        assert current_url is not None, "Test completed successfully"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: FE011-login page — QR code login flow
description: Test the QR code login flow on the login page. The login page should show a QR code option that generates a session and can be used to authenticate.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000/login and check for QR login option (button/tab)
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/login", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Click the 'Logar com QR Code' button to open the QR login flow and reveal the QR code UI.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div/div[2]/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Logar com QR Code').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('xpath=//img[contains(@src,"qrcode") or contains(@alt,"QR") or contains(@class,"qr")]').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('xpath=//*[@data-session-id or contains(@src,"session") or contains(@src,"session_id")]').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Aguardando leitura...').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('xpath=//button[contains(.,"Atualizar") or contains(.,"Refresh") or contains(.,"Recarregar")] | //*[contains(text(),"Expira") or contains(text(),"Expira em") or contains(text(),"expires")]').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
The QR login UI displays a QR code but the page does not expose session or polling indicators required to complete automated QR login verification.

Observations:
- A QR code image is visible on the login page inside a QR card.
- The page shows instructional text: 'Escaneie o código com seu celular já logado' and 'Abra o sistema no seu celular logado e autorize este acesso.'
- A 'Voltar para senha' button is present.
- No session or polling-related strings were found on the page (no 'session', 'session_id', 'sessionId', 'poll', 'Aguardando', 'Aguardando leitura', 'Aguardando autorização', 'expires', 'expira', 'refresh', 'atualizar', 'token', or 'id').
- No loading/waiting state text or any refresh/expiry indicator for the QR code is visible.
